channel.title: EU Fellowships & Traineeships
channel.description: Job listings for Fellowships roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Internship with the College of Europe in Natolin
item.description: The College of Europe in Natolin, with its postgraduate Master of Arts in European Interdisciplinary Studies (EIS), is at the cutting edge of the academic study of new developments in the EU and its neighbourhoods. It attracts students from all over Europe and beyond. We are seeking motivated and detail-oriented individuals to support wide range of our activities, including delivering the EIS academic programme, event production, campus maintenance, communication, marketing, ICT and multimedia development, and administration of projects of various scales. The internship will take place in one of the College of Europe in Natolin’s Offices or Units under the supervision of its Head. Main tasks may include: • Preparation of reports and analyses on delegated topics; • Gathering, organizing, and summarizing information and data; • Support in organisation of events and meetings; • Assisting students, professors, alumni, or guests of the College of Europe in Natolin; • Coordination of internal projects; • Creating and/or updating content on our websites and social media; • Technical support and servicing of the campus grounds and buildings; • Help with providing ICT assistance and developing ICT infrastructure. Qualifications: • Proficiency in either English or French or Polish (written and spoken); • Good organisational and communication skills; • Ability to work both independently and as part of a team with accuracy and attention to detail. About the position: • Duration: 1 – 6 months. • Start date: depending on activity and our agreement. • Location: onsite (Warsaw), remote, or hybrid, depending on activity and our agreement. • Modality: full-time or part-time, depending on activity and our agreement. • Type: remunerated civil law contract (umowa zlecenie) under Polish legislation. Application Please send your application in English or French or Polish comprising a detailed curriculum vitae and a motivation letter to internships.natolin@coleurope.eu . Should you require any further information on the advertised position, please write to the same address. The personal data provided in your application will only be processed to determine your qualifications for possible internship with the College of Europe in Natolin.
item.dc:creator: College of Europe

item.title: Alumni Relations Officer - College of Europe in Natolin
item.description: The College of Europe in Natolin is looking for a communicative and engaged Alumni Relations Officer with a starting date in September 2026. The Officer will have the possibility of shaping and maintaining relations with a community of more than 3000 alumni all over the world. About the College of Europe in Natolin Established on the initiative of the Hague Congress in 1949, the College of Europe is the world’s oldest postgraduate institute of European studies. After the end of the Cold War, in 1992 the College of Europe in Natolin began its mission to prepare students to think not only beyond disciplinary boundaries, but also beyond preconceptions, and to encourage them to build bridges and embrace a wider perspective on Europe. Ever since its foundation, the College of Europe in Natolin has been home to the academic programme of European Interdisciplinary Studies, regularly adapting its content to the changing international context and exploring emerging areas of study in European affairs. Key responsibilities As an Alumni Relations Officer, you will be responsible for building and maintaining strong, lifelong connections with our alumni community. You will coordinate regular communications, manage alumni records and engagement data, and develop outreach campaigns that keep graduates informed, connected, and actively involved with the institution. You will support the planning and delivery of alumni events, reunions, networking activities, and mentoring programmes, creating meaningful opportunities for alumni to reconnect with the College and contribute to student and campus life. Acting as a key point of contact, you will foster alumni participation through volunteering, mentoring, and other engagement initiatives. Working closely with colleagues across the institution, you will help shape and implement long-term alumni engagement strategies aligned with institutional priorities. You will monitor engagement outcomes, prepare reports, and contribute to strategic projects that strengthen alumni relationships, enhance community impact, and support the continued growth of our alumni network. Qualifications and competencies We are seeking a dynamic and relationship-oriented professional with a university degree in European Studies, Communications, Social Sciences, or a related field. The ideal candidate will possess excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, while knowledge of French or additional languages will be considered an asset. Strong interpersonal and intercultural competencies are essential to effectively engage with a diverse international alumni community. Candidates should be comfortable working with databases and digital communication tools, and demonstrate a proactive, collaborative, and solutions-focused approach. The ability to build positive relationships, take initiative, and work effectively as part of a team will be key to success in this role. Previous experience in alumni relations, stakeholder engagement, community building, or a similar field is highly appreciated. Flexibility and a willingness to travel occasionally are also required to support events, outreach activities, and the continued development of our alumni network. What we offer Intellectually stimulating international work environment; Competitive remuneration package; Active engagement in shaping Alumni Relations of the College of Europe in Natolin; Personal and professional growth in dynamic and motivated team; Contribution to the visibility and future of the Natolin offer. How to apply Please send your CV and motivation letter (in English or French) by 19 July 2026 to vro@coleurope.eu . Please note that we will contact only shortlisted candidates. Should you require any further information on the advertised position, please contact us at vro@coleurope.eu .
item.dc:creator: College of Europe

item.title: Student Recruitment Officer – College of Europe in Natolin, Warsaw
item.dc:creator: College of Europe

item.title: Alumni Relations Officer - College of Europe in Natolin, Warsaw
item.description: The College of Europe in Natolin is looking for a communicative and engaged Alumni Relations Officer with a starting date in September 2026. The Officer will have the possibility of shaping and maintaining relations with a community of more than 3000 alumni all over the world. About the College of Europe in Natolin Established on the initiative of the Hague Congress in 1949, the College of Europe is the world’s oldest postgraduate institute of European studies. After the end of the Cold War, in 1992 the College of Europe in Natolin began its mission to prepare students to think not only beyond disciplinary boundaries, but also beyond preconceptions, and to encourage them to build bridges and embrace a wider perspective on Europe. Ever since its foundation, the College of Europe in Natolin has been home to the academic programme of European Interdisciplinary Studies, regularly adapting its content to the changing international context and exploring emerging areas of study in European affairs. Key responsibilities As an Alumni Relations Officer, you will be responsible for building and maintaining strong, lifelong connections with our alumni community. You will coordinate regular communications, manage alumni records and engagement data, and develop outreach campaigns that keep graduates informed, connected, and actively involved with the institution. You will support the planning and delivery of alumni events, reunions, networking activities, and mentoring programmes, creating meaningful opportunities for alumni to reconnect with the College and contribute to student and campus life. Acting as a key point of contact, you will foster alumni participation through volunteering, mentoring, and other engagement initiatives. Working closely with colleagues across the institution, you will help shape and implement long-term alumni engagement strategies aligned with institutional priorities. You will monitor engagement outcomes, prepare reports, and contribute to strategic projects that strengthen alumni relationships, enhance community impact, and support the continued growth of our alumni network. Qualifications and competencies We are seeking a dynamic and relationship-oriented professional with a university degree in European Studies, Communications, Social Sciences, or a related field. The ideal candidate will possess excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, while knowledge of French or additional languages will be considered an asset. Strong interpersonal and intercultural competencies are essential to effectively engage with a diverse international alumni community. Candidates should be comfortable working with databases and digital communication tools, and demonstrate a proactive, collaborative, and solutions-focused approach. The ability to build positive relationships, take initiative, and work effectively as part of a team will be key to success in this role. Previous experience in alumni relations, stakeholder engagement, community building, or a similar field is highly appreciated. Flexibility and a willingness to travel occasionally are also required to support events, outreach activities, and the continued development of our alumni network. What we offer Intellectually stimulating international work environment; Competitive remuneration package; Active engagement in shaping Alumni Relations of the College of Europe in Natolin; Personal and professional growth in dynamic and motivated team; Contribution to the visibility and future of the Natolin offer. How to apply Please send your CV and motivation letter (in English or French) by 19 July 2026 to vro@coleurope.eu . Please note that we will contact only shortlisted candidates. Should you require any further information on the advertised position, please contact us at vro@coleurope.eu .
item.dc:creator: College of Europe

item.title: Student Affairs Coordinator
item.description: Short summary The College of Europe is seeking applications for the position of Student Affairs Coordinator (80-100 % FTE) , joining the Student Affairs Office at its Bruges campus from September 2026 onwards. This newly created position will act as the operational lead of the Student Affairs Office and serve as one of the primary points of contact for student-related matters outside the academic sphere. The Coordinator will supervise the Student Affairs team, support students facing complex situations and contribute to fostering a safe, inclusive and supportive campus environment. About the College The College of Europe is the oldest postgraduate institute of European studies. It brings together graduates from diverse backgrounds to study and live in an international environment across its campuses in Bruges, Natolin and Tirana. As a centre of excellence in European studies, the College is committed to contributing to European cooperation and integration . Staff members contribute to this mission through professional dedication, a spirit of proactive collegiality and mutual respect. For more information about the College, please visit our website . About the Student Affairs Office The Student Affairs Office supports students on all non-academic aspects of their experience at the Bruges campus. It plays a central role in fostering student well-being, building community life and supporting students throughout their studies. The Office works closely with student representatives, internal services and external partners to ensure that students have access to appropriate support, guidance and resources. It also contributes to the implementation of the College's Code of Conduct and supports initiatives aimed at creating a respectful and inclusive living and learning environment The Role Reporting to the Director of Student Affairs, the Student Affairs Coordinator will act as the operational lead of the Student Affairs Office. The Coordinator supervises the Student Affairs team, serves as the primary point of contact for student-related matters and ensures the effective delivery and continuous improvement of student support services. The main tasks and responsibilities of the Student Affairs Coordinator include: Lead, coach and support the Student Affairs Officer and Student Welfare Officer, ensuring high-quality service delivery and professional development; Provide guidance to the Student Affairs Officer of the Tirana branch; Serve as one of the primary points of contact for student-related matters and as the escalation point for complex, sensitive or high-impact situations, ensuring appropriate resolution and follow-up; Support students facing personal, practical, conduct or well-being-related challenges and coordinate appropriate follow-up and referrals, in close collaboration with the Student Welfare Officer; Build constructive relationships with student representatives and serve as a key liaison between students and the College administration; Coordinate the implementation of the College's Code of Conduct and contribute to prevention, awareness-raising and support initiatives; Manage student complaints and help resolving interpersonal conflicts in a professional, impartial and solution-oriented manner; Prepare and monitor the Student Affairs budget in cooperation with the Director of Student Affairs; Liaise with other College services and external stakeholders, including healthcare and well-being professionals, local authorities and partner organisations; Lead the continuous improvement of services, policies and procedures within the Student Affairs Office to ensure a high-quality student experience. Your Profile For this position, the College is looking for a mature and service-oriented professional who combines strong interpersonal skills with sound judgement, empathy and resilience. Candidates should possess: A master’s degree, preferably in social sciences, psychology, educational sciences, human resources or a related field; Minimum 5 years of relevant professional experience in higher education, student support, welfare services, human resources or comparable people-focused environments; Excellent command of English and French; knowledge of Dutch and/or other languages is considered an asset; Strong interpersonal, communication and management skills; The ability to manage difficult conversations with professionalism, empathy and appropriate assertiveness; Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced and international environment; The ability to work autonomously while contributing positively to a collaborative team environment; A high degree of integrity, discretion and respect for confidentiality. Experience or familiarity with the activities and operations of the College of Europe would be considered an asset. Our offer A pleasant and stimulating international working environment; A one-year employment contract under Belgian law, with the prospect of renewal; A competitive remuneration; A comprehensive benefits package including legal holiday pay, end-of-year bonus, meal vouchers, transportation allowance, hospitalisation insurance, and group insurance; Legal holidays plus two additional public holidays (9th of May and 11th of July) and eight closing days during the winter break; A telework policy of up to two days per week, together with a telework allowance; A unique opportunity to contribute to a renowned project of European integration. How to apply Applications in English, comprising a detailed CV (max. 2 pages) and a motivation letter (max. 1 page) should be submitted by 31 July 2026 to the HR Office at recruitment.be@coleurope.eu , mentioning “ Student Affairs Coordinator ” in the subject line. Early applications are highly encouraged. As applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, we reserve the right to close this vacancy early if a suitable candidate is found. Short-listed candidates will be invited for an interview during the week of 3 August 2026. Envisaged start date : 7 September 2026. For further information on the position, please contact Pierre Bachelier, Director Student Affairs at pierre.bachelier@coleurope.eu . For HR related questions, please contact Julie Favril, HR Co-Director at recruitment.be@coleurope.eu . *** By submitting your application, you consent to have your personal data processed for the purpose of recruitment and the specific position you are applying for. You acknowledge that your data, as well as data we receive from academic institutions we can contact for verification purposes, will be processed by the College of Europe, the members of the selection committee appointed by the College of Europe related to this recruitment process. All information will be treated with the utmost confidentiality and in line with the current data processing regulations. For more information on how we use the information you have provided and how to exercise your rights, please see our privacy notice for recruitment ( https://www.coleurope.eu/privacy ). *** The College of Europe is committed to equal treatment and opportunity. It aims to reflect the diversity of our society and to offer an inclusive work and study environment for all members of its community. Consistent with these goals, the Bruges campus has a zero-tolerance policy for any acts against the dignity and integrity of individuals, whether in the form of discrimination, bullying, harassment, stalking, violence, or sexual misconduct. Candidates are therefore recruited and selected on the basis of skills and experience, regardless of sex, age, ability, ethnicity, class and background, political belief, race, religion, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, culture, or any other grounds.
item.dc:creator: College of Europe
